title: Strict TSConfig, TypeScript 7 Defaults, and Project Graphs · Contract Defense
summary: Create a clean multi-package configuration that fails on unsafe boundaries, unused work, and incompatible module assumptions. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a strict TypeScript 7 project configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.
Predict root files from files, include, exclude, imports, types, and library selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 2. Competency probe: Use showConfig, traceResolution, explainFiles, clean builds, and fixed parallelism to diagnose configuration rather than guess.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
